podcasts.name: Davie Fogarty
podcasts.title: You don’t have to be good to start
podcasts.transcript: I was in school and I was [music] told that I was really bad at English. Then I studied really hard at English and I got an academic award and I went off on stage and my friends basically called out, said, "He can't even read." Then I was told I was bad at math. So I enrolled in the hardest math class in school. I've been told multiple times that I'm bad at endurance because I've always been like fast and strong. I find this great pleasure in like finding the things that I'm bad at. The order can be created from chaos by simple hard work. There's only so many things that we can control in this world and we're going to be dealt so many things that we can't control that I almost crave things that are not true, but with pure hard work I can create that order and I can control it. And this is a perfect example. Like literally probably two, three years ago I would have been barely able to run 10 Ks and I was so, so slow because I was big and I was heavy. I could barely swim literally six months ago. I definitely hadn't ridden a bike. You can see footage of me on my Instagram where I'm trying to click on the pedals and I fall over. So it's just about doing hard At the end of the day, I think too many people are scared to do like events like this because they see people on social media post crazy times. Then they try once, they go for a run and they're like, "Why aren't I running 450 Ks like this famous influencer?" Where their whole job is to be a fitness athlete. Like that is all they do. People get turned away from it. I'm trying to do the thing. I don't care about the time. It's just about doing the thing and being proud of it, I guess.
